title: thymus serpyllum subsp. angustifolius
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. angustifolius, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like thymol, carvacrol, and geraniol, with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to aid in digestion, reduce stress, and promote wound healing. The plant can be used to make herbal teas, essential oils, and infusions, which are commonly prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water or by distilling its essential oil through steam.
